response:
Nurse Emily has organized a creative writing workshop for 24 residents, dividing them into groups of 6. This results in 4 groups in total. Each group will receive 3 writing prompts initially, and an additional 10 prompts will be shared equally among all groups. 

The initial prompts are distributed as $ 4 \text{ groups} \times 3 \text{ prompts per group} = 12 $ prompts. The extra prompts are shared equally, so $ 10 \text{ prompts} \div 4 \text{ groups} = 2.5 \text{ prompts per group} $. Since prompts must be whole numbers, we round up to ensure each group has at least 2 extra prompts. This means 3 extra prompts are given to each group, resulting in $ 4 \times 3 = 12 $ extra prompts.

In total, Emily needs $ 12 + 12 = 24 $ writing prompts. The library provides resources that support the creative process, and the library also offers a variety of writing tools that can be used to enhance the session.

The END.
